content: I know there's work about the tensor product and hom and (small) biproducts of presentable categories; is there something about the further linear algebra (modules and tensors over monoids, e.g.)? I recently worked out that a commutative monoid in presentable 1-categories seems to be a presentable [[Benabou cosmos]] and a module, a copowered and powered enrichment of a presentable category. It sounds interesting, and surely someone must have noticed before.

Yes, there's definitely work in this area since these kinds of categorified monoid actions are very important for topological quantum field theories. One good reference that you might already know is the "EGNO" book Tensor Categories, which is not in the presentable setting but still shows what kinds of things one can do with categorified modules/etc. It's my impression that in the presentable case a lot of this stuff can be found in at least one of Lurie's books (probably higher algebra, as a special case of something to do with operads?) but I'm not actually 100% sure on that
